content.claim: Hungary tracks students at ages 10-11 (Grade 5) or 12-13 (Grade 7) into 6- and 8-year gimnázium programs, significantly earlier than the OECD average of age 15, with approximately 85% of Hungarian students in schools that select by academic performance or recommendation compared to an OECD average of 43%.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: OECD comparative data does not explain Hungarian-specific institutional rationale
Selection mechanisms function competitively despite formal 'free school choice' framing
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
